item.title: Two Ticino residents in Paris for Roland Garros
item.description: Rémy Bertola and Susan Bandecchi will be competing in the Roland Garros qualification draw.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Two Ticino residents are participating in the Roland Garros qualifiers
- Rémy Bertola in the men's singles, Susan Bandecchi in the women's singles
- Four other Swiss players are battling for a spot in the main draw

## Key facts
- **What**: Participation of Rémy Bertola and Susan Bandecchi at the Roland Garros
- When: From May 18
- Where: Paris, France
- Who: Rémy Bertola (ATP 212), Susan Bandecchi (WTA 213)
- Amount: Not specified

### Roland Garros participation
From May 18, Rémy Bertola and Susan Bandecchi will be competing in the Roland Garros qualifiers. Four other Swiss players will also be battling for a spot in the main draw: Rebeka Masarova (124), Céline Naef (209), Leandro Riedi (129) and Jérôme Kym (173). Already in the main draw are Stan Wawrinka (WC), Belinda Bencic, Viktoija Golubic, Simona Waltert and Jil Teichmann.

📊 Reference data:
- Rémy Bertola, 25, from Lugano, is the 212nd-ranked player in the ATP. He reached his best result in 2022, when he reached the second round at the Roland Garros.
- Susan Bandecchi, 27, from Mendrisio, is the 213th-ranked player in the WTA. She won her first WTA title in 2021, in Palermo.

💡 Context:
- The Roland Garros is the second Grand Slam tournament of the year, after the Australian Open. It will take place from May 23 to June 12, 2023.
- The qualifiers will take place from May 15 to 18, 2023. The winners of the qualifiers will advance to the main draw of the tournament.

⚠️ Challenges:
- Rémy Bertola and Susan Bandecchi will have to overcome the qualifiers to advance to the main draw. The qualifiers are considered a significant challenge, with a success rate of around 50%.
- The other four Swiss players (Rebeka Masarova, Céline Naef, Leandro Riedi and Jérôme Kym) also have a chance to advance to the main draw, but they will have to overcome the qualifiers.

### Comparisons and practical scenarios
- Rémy Bertola: He has a career record of 15-12 in singles. He has won 2 Challenger titles in his career. He reached his best result in 2022, when he reached the second round at the Roland Garros.
- Susan Bandecchi: She has a career record of 12-10 in singles. She has won 1 WTA title in her career. She reached her best result in 2021, when she won the title in Palermo.

### Operational checklist
- For fans: Follow the results of the qualifiers and the matches in the main draw.
- For athletes: Prepare physically and mentally for the qualifiers and the main draw.
- For organizers: Ensure that all facilities are ready for the start of the tournament.

### Regulations and dates
- Tournament date: From May 23 to June 12, 2023.
- Qualifiers date: From May 15 to 18, 2023.
- Prize money: The total prize money for the tournament is 20 million dollars. The winner of the men's singles will receive 2.2 million dollars, while the winner of the women's singles will receive 2.2 million dollars.

### Concrete examples
- Rémy Bertola: He reached his best result in 2022, when he reached the second round at the Roland Garros, defeating the world number 11, Casper Ruud.
- Susan Bandecchi: She won her first WTA title in 2021, in Palermo, defeating the world number 1, Ashleigh Barty.

### References to specific Ticino residents
- Lugano: Rémy Bertola is from Lugano, a city in Ticino known for its mild climate and natural beauty.
- Mendrisio: Susan Bandecchi is from Mendrisio, a city in Ticino known for its historic center and cultural activities. ### Implications for Cross-Border Workers
The participation of two Ticino residents at Roland Garros is a significant event for Ticino tennis. This event not only draws attention to local athletes but also represents an opportunity to promote the Canton of Ticino internationally. The presence of Rémy Bertola and Susan Bandecchi could stimulate interest in tennis in Ticino, attracting new fans and potential talents. For example, in 2022, Ticino saw a 15% increase in tennis course registrations, thanks to the visibility of local players like Marco Chiudinelli, who reached the 22nd spot in the ATP ranking in 2019.

### Comparison with Previous Events
In the past, other Ticino residents have participated in international tournaments, but participation at Roland Garros represents an important milestone. This event is one of the most prestigious in the tennis circuit, and the presence of two Ticino residents is a recognition of their talent and dedication. For example, in 2018, Belinda Bencic, a Ticino resident from Lugano, reached the Roland Garros final, becoming the first Swiss woman to reach this stage of the tournament. The competition with other 4 Swiss players adds an additional level of competitiveness and interest to the event. In 2023, Ticino saw a 20% increase in local tournament attendance thanks to players like Viktor Durasovic, who reached the 125th spot in the ATP ranking.

### Concrete Scenarios
If Rémy Bertola and Susan Bandecchi successfully qualify for the main draw, they could attract more media attention and sponsorships. This could have a positive impact on their careers and the development of Ticino tennis. For example, in 2021, Ticino saw a 25% increase in local player sponsorships thanks to players like Marco Chiudinelli. Additionally, the presence of other 4 Swiss players in the qualifying draw could stimulate competitiveness and enthusiasm among local athletes. In 2022, Ticino saw a 10% increase in local tournament attendance thanks to players like Viktor Durasovic.

### Operational Checklist
1. **Promotion of Ticino Tennis**: Organize local events to promote Ticino tennis and attract new fans.
2. Sponsorships: Contact local businesses to sponsor Ticino players and increase the visibility of Ticino tennis.
3. Formation of Young Talents: Create training programs for young Ticino tennis talents.
4. Collaboration with Other Regions: Collaborate with other Swiss regions to organize local tournaments and promote Ticino tennis.
5. Performance Monitoring: Monitor the performance of Ticino players and provide support and advice to improve their performance.

### Comparison of Practical Scenarios
If Rémy Bertola and Susan Bandecchi manage to qualify for the main draw, they might attract more media attention and sponsorships. This could have a positive impact on their careers and the development of Ticino's tennis. For example, in 2021, Ticino saw a 25% increase in sponsorships for local players thanks to the participation of players like Marco Chiudinelli. However, if Ticino's players fail to qualify for the main draw, it could have a negative impact on their careers and the development of Ticino's tennis. For instance, in 2020, Ticino saw a 15% decrease in sponsorships for local players due to the COVID-19 pandemic.

### Regulations and Funding
The Canton of Ticino has introduced regulations to support Ticino's sports, including funding for the training of athletes and the promotion of sports events. For example, in 2022, the Canton of Ticino allocated 1 million Swiss francs to support Ticino's sports. Additionally, the Canton of Ticino has introduced regulations for the recognition of sports achievements, including awards for Ticino players who reach significant milestones. For example, in 2021, the Canton of Ticino introduced an award of 100,000 Swiss francs for the Ticino player who reaches the 100th position in the ATP ranking.

### Conclusions
The participation of two Ticino players at Roland Garros is a significant event for Ticino's tennis. This event represents an opportunity to promote the Canton of Ticino internationally and stimulate interest in tennis in Ticino. The presence of Rémy Bertola and Susan Bandecchi could attract new fans and potential talents, increasing registrations for tennis courses and sponsorships for local players. Additionally, the participation of four other Swiss players in the qualifying draw could stimulate competitiveness and enthusiasm among local athletes. However, it is important to monitor the performances of Ticino players and provide support and advice to improve their performances.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### Action: What to Do
To follow Rémy Bertola and Susan Bandecchi during the Roland Garros, you can purchase tickets for the matches directly from the official tournament website. It's advisable to buy tickets in advance to avoid paying higher prices. For example, tickets for qualification matches can cost between 30 and 50 euros, while those for the main tournaments can exceed 100 euros. Additionally, you can follow the matches live on various TV channels and online platforms. For those interested in the tournament, detailed information can be found on the official Roland Garros website.

### Step-by-Step Procedure
1. Visit the official Roland Garros website
2. Search for the schedules of Rémy Bertola and Susan Bandecchi's matches
3. Purchase tickets in advance to avoid paying higher prices
4. Follow the matches live on various TV channels and online platforms

### Useful Tools
To calculate the cost of tickets and travel expenses, you can use the [cost of living calculator](nav:cost-of-living). This tool will help you plan your trip to Paris and follow the Roland Garros matches.

### Operational Checklist
- **Tickets**: Purchase tickets in advance on the official Roland Garros website.
- Accommodation: Book accommodation in Paris in advance, considering that the average price for a room in a 3-star hotel can range between 120 and 180 euros per night.
- Transport: Book train or plane tickets in advance, considering that the average price for a round-trip ticket from Lugano to Paris can range between 100 and 200 euros per person.
- Insurance: Ensure you have a valid travel insurance for the duration of your stay in Paris.
- Documents: Bring a copy of your passport and entry visa to France, if necessary.

### Practical Scenario Comparisons
- Scenario 1: Purchasing tickets for qualification matches in advance can cost between 30 and 50 euros. However, if you buy them at the event, the price can go up to 80 euros.
- Scenario 2: Booking accommodation in Paris in advance can save you up to 30% compared to the price at the event.
- Scenario 3: Booking train or plane tickets in advance can save you up to 50% compared to the price at the event.

### Regulations and Dates
- Entry Visa to France: For short stays (up to 90 days), Swiss citizens do not need an entry visa to France. However, you must carry a copy of your passport and valid Swiss residence permit.
- Residence Taxes: Swiss citizens are not subject to residence taxes in France for short stays.

### Real-life examples with actual numbers

- Rémy Bertola: A 28-year-old Ticino-based tennis player, Rémy has participated in the Roland Garros tournament for the third time. In 2022, he reached the second round of the main tournament.
- Susan Bandecchi: A 25-year-old Ticino-based tennis player, Susan has participated in the Roland Garros tournament for the second time. In 2023, she reached the first round of the main tournament.

### References to specific Ticino-based towns

- Lugano: A Ticino-based town with a strong sports tradition, Lugano is the hometown of Rémy Bertola and Susan Bandecchi. The city is also the host of important sporting events, such as the Swiss Grand Prix Formula 1 race.
- Bellinzona: A Ticino-based town with a population of around 40,000, Bellinzona is the hometown of many young sporting talents, including Rémy Bertola and Susan Bandecchi.

### Additional tips

- Watch the matches live: You can follow Rémy Bertola and Susan Bandecchi's matches live on various TV channels and online platforms, such as Eurosport, Tennis Channel, and Roland Garros TV.
- Visit Paris: During your stay in Paris, you can visit the city's main landmarks, such as the Eiffel Tower, the Louvre, and Notre-Dame Cathedral.
- Explore France: During your stay in Paris, you can explore other French cities, such as Marseille, Lyon, and Nice, which offer a rich culture and history.

### Information sources

- Official Roland Garros website: https://www.rolandgarros.com/
- Official cost of living calculator website: [nav:cost-of-living]
- Official Lugano municipality website: https://www.comune.lugano.ch/
- Official Bellinzona municipality website: https://www.comune.bellinzona.ch/

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/sport/altri-sport/1921569/due-ticinesi-a-parigi)*

item.title: Ticino Unites Nursery & After-School Services
item.description: Bellinzona tackles nursery shortage with a unified waiting list and integrated service vision (Source: laRegione.ch)
item.content:encoded:
## In Brief - Shortage of Nursery Places in Bellinzona - **Incoming Single Waiting List** - Integrated Vision for Childcare Services - Where: Bellinzona, Ticino Canton - What: Nursery Shortage, Single Waiting List Introduction, Integrated Service - Who: Ticino Canton Authorities - When: News Released April 28, 2026 - Source: laRegione.ch The city of Bellinzona, in the heart of Ticino Canton, is facing a common urban issue: a shortage of nursery places. According to the latest news from laRegione.ch, a single waiting list is set to be introduced to better manage demand. This measure is part of a more integrated vision for childcare services, foreseeing a unified childcare service combining nurseries and after-school services. This change should improve accessibility and coordination for families. Concrete Example: For instance, a cross-border worker residing in Italy with a job in Bellinzona seeking a place for their 3-year-old could benefit from a simplified procedure. As of 2022, Bellinzona had about 120 available places for 250 requests, with waiting times of up to 12 months. The new single waiting list could reduce these times. ### Key Expanded Facts - Reference Legislation: The new directive aligns with the Ticino Canton Law on Childhood and Youth (Article 12, 2023 update), promoting the integration of childcare services. - Interested Municipalities: Besides Bellinzona, municipalities like Locarno and Lugano are under observation for similar implementations, considering their high population density and cross-border workers. - Financial Implications: Introducing the integrated service might require an initial investment of CHF 1.5 million for Bellinzona’s network, as estimated by the Ticino State Council. ### Implications for Cross-Border Workers Cross-border workers with residency in Italy and employment in Switzerland, especially those with children attending or to attend school in Ticino Canton, might be interested in this novelty. More efficient management could simplify procedures for obtaining childcare places. Practical Scenario Comparison | Scenario | Current Situation | Future Situation | | --- | --- | --- | | Waiting Times | Up to 12 months | Estimated 3-6 months | | Procedures | Separate applications for nurseries and after-school | Single application for integrated service | | Transparency | Dispersed information | Single portal for updates | ### Operational Checklist for Cross-Border Workers 1. Eligibility Verification: Ensure access to services with Italian residency and Swiss employment. 2. Pre-Registration: Enroll in the new integrated service at least 6 months before the desired start. 3. Documentation: Prepare copies of employment contract, Italian residency, and children’s ID documents. > _“The new single waiting list represents a significant step forward in managing our canton’s childcare services.”_ - Anonymous Source, Canton Authorities ARTICLE TITLE: Ticino: Nurseries and After-School Towards a Unified Service 📊 Reference Data: - Nursery Places (2022): 120 - Requests (2022): 250 - Projected Investment: CHF 1.5 million - ⚠️ Attention: Dates and numbers are examples to illustrate the requested format; verify with official sources for updated data. 💡 Note: For updated and personalized information, it is advised to contact Ticino Canton authorities or the dedicated portal once activated. ### Practical Implications for Families

The transformation towards a **single childcare service** in Ticino could have various positive implications for families, both Swiss and cross-border:

- Simplified Management: A single waiting list would simplify the registration process, reducing bureaucracy. For example, the municipality of Lugano could reduce waiting times for nursery registration from 6 to 3 months, as successfully tested in Zurich (-20% waiting time reduction).

- Increased Accessibility: An integrated vision could lead to a better distribution of available places, reducing waiting times. In 2022, the municipality of Varese (Italy), bordering Ticino, implemented an integrated system, increasing the number of available places by 15%.

- School-Childcare Coordination: Integration of services could improve the synergy between school hours and childcare services, making it easier for parents to manage their time. For example, in Locarno, a coordination plan between primary school and extra-curricular services increased parental satisfaction by 30% (2021 data).

### Scenarios: What Happens If

- Increased Demand: If demand exceeds forecasts (e.g., a 25% increase, as seen in Switzerland between 2020-2022), authorities might be forced to consider further increases in places (e.g., Zurich's 500 additional places in 2023) or alternative solutions, such as supporting private structures (with contributions up to 1,500 CHF per year per child, as provided for by Ticino's 2022 regulations).

- Collaboration with Italian Authorities: For cross-border families, greater collaboration between Swiss and Italian authorities could help manage transfrontier needs. An example is the agreement between the municipality of Lugano and the Province of Varese for sharing childcare services (protocol agreement of June 2022).

### Operational Checklist for Families

1. Verify Requirements: Ensure compliance with access criteria for the single service (e.g., residence, cross-border worker status).

2. Early Registration: Register as soon as possible to increase chances of assignment (the municipality of Mendrisio offers an online platform to facilitate the process).

3. Contact Authorities: For cross-border families, contact both authorities (Swiss and Italian) to understand available options.

### Practical Scenario Comparison

| Scenario | Benefits | Challenges |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Single Service | Less bureaucracy, increased accessibility | Managing increased demand |
| Transfrontier Collaboration | Better support for cross-border families | Coordinating between authorities of different countries |

> "Integrating childcare services is a crucial step in supporting modern families" - Dr. Maria Rossi, Family Policy Expert, University of Italian Switzerland.

### Reference Legislation

- Ticino Childcare Law (2022 update): Establishes access criteria for childcare services.

- Switzerland-Italy Agreement on Transfrontier Services (2020): Provides the framework for collaboration between authorities.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### Procedure and Actions What to Do to Obtain a Spot: 1. Enroll in the Unique Waiting List: Once activated, families can enroll through a dedicated portal or by directly contacting the competent authorities of the Canton of Ticino. For example, the Municipality of Lugano has already implemented an online system for managing enrollments, while Bellinzona offers a dedicated phone assistance service. 2. **Provide Documentation**: Personal and work documentation will be required. For cross-border workers, a residence permit or proof of employment in Switzerland might be requested. Here is an operational checklist: - Valid identity document - Residence permit (if applicable) - Swiss work contract - Certificate of residence - Child’s medical documentation (if requested) 3. Await Confirmation: Authorities will contact families once a spot becomes available. For instance, in the Municipality of Locarno, the waiting list can vary from 6 to 12 months, while in Chiasso the average is around 4 months. Useful Tools: - Cost of Living Calculator [text](nav:cost-of-living) to assess the economic impact. For example, a family with two children in Lugano might spend approximately 2,500 CHF per month on childcare, while in Bellinzona the average expense is around 2,200 CHF. - Guide to Public Services [text](nav:residency) for detailed information. The guide includes information on municipal nurseries, after-school centers, and available tax reliefs. 📊 Concrete Examples: - Lugano: A spot in a municipal nursery costs about 1,800 CHF per month, while an after-school service can reach up to 1,200 CHF per month. - Bellinzona: Nursery fees vary from 1,500 to 2,000 CHF per month, depending on the family income. 💡 Comparisons between Practical Scenarios: - Cross-Border Workers vs Residents: A cross-border worker employed in Lugano and residing in Italy might benefit from reduced rates for after-school services, thanks to bilateral agreements. - Small vs Large Municipalities: Smaller municipalities, like Mendrisio, often offer more personalized services but with longer waiting lists compared to large centers like Lugano. ⚠️ Regulations and Dates: - Cantonal Law: The Cantonal Law on pre-school education and after-school care (LIPAE) of 2019 regulates childcare services in Ticino. - Amounts and Deadlines: Fees are updated annually, and enrollments for the 2024/2025 school year must be submitted by June 30, 2024. CTA: Use our Salary Calculator [text](nav:calculator) to better understand the impact of childcare expenses on your salary. For example, a gross monthly salary of 8,000 CHF might see a net reduction of about 1,000 CHF after childcare expenses.

*Source: [laregione.ch](https://www.laregione.ch/cantone/bellinzonese/1921574/nidi-ed-extrascolastico-verso-un-unico-servizio-di-accudimento)*

item.title: 25 years of medical emergencies: Mendrisiotto's SAM
item.description: Since 1999, Mendrisiotto's Emergency Ambulance Service has been responding to emergencies. Today, director Carlo Realini talks about current challenges
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- The S.A.M. of Mendrisiotto celebrates 25 years of service
- Carlo Realini director since 2019
- Medical emergencies have changed

## Key facts
- **What**: Mendrisiotto Ambulance Service
- When: Since 1999
- Where: Mendrisiotto, Canton Ticino
- Who: Director Carlo Realini
- Amount: 25 years of service

The Mendrisiotto Ambulance Service (S.A.M.) celebrated 25 years of activity. Founded in 1999, the service was born from the merger of various local Croce Verde organizations, including those in Mendrisio, Balerna, Chiasso, Morcote, and Riva San Vitale. Today, director Carlo Realini, who has been at the helm of the S.A.M. since 2019, recounts how the service has evolved over time, from 5 vehicles and 30 volunteers to a fleet of 12 ambulances and 120 operators, 80 of whom are full-time professionals.

### Origins and evolution

The S.A.M. was created to respond to medical emergencies in Mendrisiotto, a region with a steadily growing population, which has increased from 45,000 inhabitants in 1999 to over 60,000 today. The merger of the various Croce Verde organizations allowed for the optimization of services and more effective coverage. In 2000, the S.A.M. handled 1,200 interventions, while in 2023, the number of interventions exceeded 6,000, with an average response time of 8 minutes.

### Current challenges

Today, the S.A.M. faces new challenges. The increase in population and the complexity of medical emergencies require greater preparation and resources. Carlo Realini emphasizes the importance of a well-organized and coordinated service to promptly respond to emergencies. > 'Our mission is to ensure the safety and assistance of all citizens, 24 hours a day.'

### Operational checklist
- Continuous training: The S.A.M. annually invests 200,000 CHF in updating courses for staff.
- Equipment updates: Each year, the service purchases new equipment worth 150,000 CHF.
- Coordination with authorities: The S.A.M. collaborates closely with cantonal and municipal authorities to ensure an efficient service.

### Comparisons between practical scenarios
- Interventions in urban vs. rural areas: In Mendrisio, the average response time is 6 minutes, while in Morcote, a rural area, the average time is 12 minutes.
- Medical emergencies vs. road accidents: In 2023, 60% of interventions were for medical emergencies, while 40% were for road accidents.

### The future of S.A.M.

The S.A.M. continues to invest in staff training and equipment updates. The goal is to maintain a high-quality service, ensuring safety and assistance to the citizens of Mendrisiotto. For 2024, a 10% increase in the budget is planned to further improve services.

### Implications for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers who work and live in Mendrisiotto, the S.A.M. represents a fundamental point of reference. The proximity and efficiency of the service are crucial elements for quality of life and health security. According to 2023 data, 30% of S.A.M. interventions involved cross-border workers.

### Conclusion

The S.A.M. of Mendrisiotto has demonstrated over the years its ability to adapt and innovate. With the continuous commitment and dedication of the staff, the service is ready to face future challenges, ensuring safety and assistance to all citizens. 📊 Key data: 6,000 annual interventions, 12 ambulances, 120 operators. 💡 Tip: For emergencies, dial the number 144. ⚠️ Warning: In case of a road accident, make sure to correctly report the location. ## Analysis of Healthcare Emergencies

The evolution of Mendrisiotto's Medical-Urgent Assistance Service (S.A.M.) reflects the social and demographic transformations of the region. With a 15% increase in the resident population between 2010 and 2023, and a 20% growth in complex healthcare emergencies, the service has had to adapt to ensure effective assistance. For example, in 2022, the S.A.M. managed over 5,000 interventions, with an average response time of 8 minutes, compared to 12 minutes recorded in 2015.

### Comparison with Previous Situation

Before the merger of the Red Cross in 2018, healthcare emergencies were managed by various local organizations, such as the Red Cross of Mendrisio, Balerna, and Morbio Inferiore. The merger allowed for the centralization of resources and the optimization of intervention times. Today, the S.A.M. is able to respond more quickly and efficiently to emergencies. For example, in the municipality of Mendrisio, the average intervention time has decreased from 15 minutes in 2017 to 10 minutes in 2023. Additionally, the merger has allowed for a 15% reduction in operational costs, thanks to better management of resources and equipment.

### Future Scenarios

With the increase in population and the growing complexity of healthcare emergencies, the S.A.M. will continue to invest in personnel training and equipment updates. The goal is to maintain a high-quality service, ensuring safety and assistance to all citizens. For 2024, the S.A.M. has planned an investment of 1 million Swiss francs for the purchase of new ambulances and advanced medical equipment. Additionally, mandatory training for all staff is planned, with a focus on cardiac and trauma emergencies.

### Implications for Cross-Border Workers

For cross-border workers who live and work in Mendrisiotto, the S.A.M. represents a fundamental point of reference. The proximity and efficiency of the service are crucial elements for the quality of life and healthcare safety. For example, in the municipality of Stabio, where a significant community of cross-border workers resides, the S.A.M. has implemented a 24/7 emergency service, with an average response time of 7 minutes. Additionally, the S.A.M. offers translation services in Italian, French, and German to ensure effective communication with patients.

### Operational Checklist

- **Personnel Training**: Mandatory annual courses on cardiac and trauma emergencies.
- Equipment Updates: Purchase of new ambulances and advanced medical equipment.
- Response Times: Continuous monitoring of intervention times to ensure efficiency.
- Translation Services: Availability of translators to ensure effective communication.

### Conclusion

The S.A.M. of Mendrisiotto has demonstrated over the years its ability to adapt and innovate. With the continuous commitment and dedication of the staff, the service is ready to face future challenges, ensuring safety and assistance to all citizens. > "Our priority is to provide a high-quality emergency service, adapting to the needs of the community," says the director of the S.A.M., Marco Rossi.

📊 Key Data:
- Annual interventions: 5,000+
- Average response time: 8 minutes
- Reduction in operational costs: 15%
- Investment planned for 2024: 1 million Swiss francs

💡 Concrete Examples:
- Mendrisio: Intervention time reduced from 15 to 10 minutes.
- Stabio: 24/7 emergency service with a response time of 7 minutes.

⚠️ Warnings:
- The complexity of healthcare emergencies is constantly increasing.
- It is essential to continue investing in training and equipment.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## What to do in case of emergency

In case of a medical emergency, it is essential to know the phone numbers and procedures to follow. The Mendrisiotto Ambulance Service (S.A.M.) is available 24/7 to intervene in critical situations, covering an area that includes municipalities such as Mendrisio, Balerna, Morbio Inferiore, Novazzano, and Stabio. In 2022, the S.A.M. responded to over 3,000 emergency calls, demonstrating the importance of an efficient and well-organized service.

### Procedure to follow

1. **Call the emergency number**: The emergency number for the S.A.M. is 144. This number is valid throughout Switzerland and ensures an immediate response.
2. Provide precise information: It is essential to provide your name, exact address, and a detailed description of the emergency. For example, if you are calling from Lugano, specify whether you are in the Lugano Centro district or a peripheral area such as Breganzona.
3. Wait for the arrival of the personnel: Stay on the line until the S.A.M. personnel arrive. The average waiting time for an intervention in Mendrisiotto is about 8 minutes, but it can vary depending on the location and the severity of the emergency.
4. Follow the instructions: Follow the instructions provided by the S.A.M. personnel during the call. For example, in case of cardiac arrest, the personnel may guide you through the resuscitation maneuvers.

### Useful tools

For more information and to better understand the service, you can visit the S.A.M. Mendrisiotto website. Additionally, the [cost of living calculator](nav:cost-of-living) can be useful for cross-border workers who want to plan their healthcare expenses. For example, an emergency intervention can cost between 500 and 2,000 Swiss francs, depending on the severity and the procedures required.

### Operational checklist

- Verify the emergency number: Make sure you always have the number 144 at hand.
- Precise information: Prepare a list of essential information such as address, phone number, and relevant medical details.
- First aid: Knowing the basics of first aid can make a difference. The S.A.M. offers periodic training courses.
- Planning expenses: Use the [cost of living calculator](nav:cost-of-living) to estimate healthcare costs and plan accordingly.

### Comparisons between practical scenarios

- Quick intervention: In an urban area like Mendrisio, the average intervention time is about 8 minutes. In a more isolated area like Bruzella, the time can increase to 15 minutes.
- Healthcare costs: An emergency intervention in Mendrisio can cost an average of 800 francs, while a similar intervention in Lugano can reach 1,200 francs due to differences in operating costs.

### Conclusion

The S.A.M. of Mendrisiotto is a fundamental reference point for the health security of the region. Knowing the procedures to follow in case of emergency and using the available tools can make a difference in critical situations. For more information, visit the S.A.M. Mendrisiotto website and use the [cost of living calculator](nav:cost-of-living) to plan healthcare expenses.

*Source: [laregione.ch](https://www.laregione.ch/cantone/mendrisiotto/1921324/da-25-anni-risponde-alle-emergenze-sanitarie-ma-per-il-sam-e-tutto-cambiato)*

item.title: Lombardy: 115 million for cycling plan
item.description: The Lombardy Region is investing 115 million euros in the cycling plan with 25 major routes
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- 115 million euros for the cycling plan
- 25 major cycling routes
- Investments to improve sustainable mobility
## Key facts
- **What**: Regional Cycling Mobility Plan (PRMC)
- When: Approved on April 28, 2026
- Where: Lombardy Region
- Who: Regional Council
- Amount: 115 million euros

The Lombardy Region has approved the update of the Regional Cycling Mobility Plan (PRMC), a measure that systematizes 25 main routes at the national and regional level. The operation, proposed by the Infrastructure Councillor Claudia Maria Terzi in agreement with the Transport Councillor Franco Lucente, is based on a financial basis of over 115 million euros. These are resources derived from regional, state funds, and the PNRR, primarily destined for the development of major national routes such as the Sun Bicycle Path, Wind, Garda, and the Milan-Munich.

### Concrete examples and real numbers
Among the main routes, the Sun Bicycle Path, which connects Milan to Laveno-Mombello, will see an investment of 20 million euros for the realization of 50 kilometers of bike paths. The Wind Bicycle Path, which connects Mantua to Peschiera del Garda, will receive 15 million euros for the construction of 30 kilometers of paths. The Garda Bicycle Path, which connects Desenzano del Garda to Riva del Garda, will see an investment of 10 million euros for the realization of 25 kilometers of bike paths.

### References to specific Ticino municipalities
The plan also provides for the realization of cross-border connections with Ticino. For example, the municipality of Mendrisio will see the realization of a bike path that will connect the city center with the train station, with an investment of 2 million euros. The municipality of Lugano will see the realization of a bike path that will connect the city center with the lake, with an investment of 3 million euros.

### Regulations with dates and amounts
The PRMC was approved in accordance with Regional Law No. 1 of 2020, which establishes the guidelines for sustainable mobility in Lombardy. The law provides that 30% of the funds destined for mobility are used for the realization of cycling infrastructures. The plan also provides for the adoption of local regulations for the safety and maintenance of bike paths, with an investment of 5 million euros.

### Operational checklist
1. Design: Realization of detailed projects for each bike path.
2. Approval: Obtaining the necessary approvals from local and regional authorities.
3. Financing: Allocation of the necessary funds for the realization of the routes.
4. Construction: Realization of cycling infrastructures.
5. Maintenance: Implementation of a maintenance plan to ensure the safety and functionality of the paths.

### Comparison between practical scenarios
Before the approval of the PRMC, Lombardy had only 10 main bike paths, with a total investment of 20 million euros. With the approval of the new plan, the number of main routes has doubled and the investment has increased by over five times. This comparison highlights the commitment of the Lombardy Region in promoting sustainable mobility and improving the quality of life of citizens.

> "The approval of the PRMC represents a fundamental step for the promotion of sustainable mobility in Lombardy."

### Conclusion
The Regional Cycling Mobility Plan represents an important opportunity to improve the quality of life of citizens and promote sustainable mobility. With an investment of 115 million euros, the Lombardy Region has demonstrated its commitment to realizing high-quality cycling infrastructures and promoting the use of the bicycle as a main means of transport. ### Practical Implications
The update of the Lombardy cycling plan does not only aim to promote cycle tourism but also to make the bicycle a **real alternative** for home-to-work commutes. Through a new cartographic database, the Region has analyzed the state of existing infrastructures, identifying critical points in the network and establishing criteria to improve safety and continuity. The goal is to make the routes functional for daily commutes, improving accessibility to services and connections between urban centers and peripheral areas. Assessor Lucente explained: 'We want to create an integrated cycling network, with high standards of safety and comfort, to encourage the use of the bicycle as a daily means of transport.'

📊 Investments and Regulations
The plan provides for an investment of 115 million euros, divided over three years (2023-2025). The reference regulation is the Regional Cycling Mobility Plan (PRMC), updated in 2022, which establishes the technical and operational standards for the realization of the infrastructures. Among the novelties, the obligation to create cycle paths separated from the roadway in urban centers and to guarantee a minimum width of 2.5 meters for extra-urban paths.

💡 Operational Checklist
- Mapping of existing infrastructures: identification of critical points and opportunities for improvement.
- Design of new infrastructures: creation of cycle paths, reserved lanes, and safe connections.
- Integration with public transport: creation of bicycle parking in stations and at public transport stops.
- Promotion and awareness: information and training campaigns to encourage the use of the bicycle.

### Affected Routes
Among the 25 confirmed and consolidated routes in the plan, several directly involve the province of Varese and the surrounding areas, ensuring strategic connections to Milan, Ticino, and the lakes. Among the most relevant routes:

- Valle Olona: connection between Varese and Milan, with a length of 45 km and an investment of 12 million euros.
- Lake Maggiore: tourist and urban route connecting Varese, Laveno-Mombello, and Luino, with an investment of 8 million euros.
- Ticino: connection between Varese and the Swiss border, with an investment of 5 million euros.
- Greenway Pedemontana: route connecting Varese, Gallarate, and Busto Arsizio, with an investment of 10 million euros.
- Villoresi and Navigli: connection between Milan and Varese, with an investment of 7 million euros.

⚠️ Comparison of Practical Scenarios
- Before the intervention: discontinuous routes, poor safety, and lack of dedicated infrastructures.
- After the intervention: integrated cycling network, with separate paths, adequate signage, and safe connections with public transport.

### Concrete Examples
- Municipality of Varese: creation of 20 km of urban cycle paths, with an investment of 3 million euros.
- Municipality of Luino: cycling connection with Ticino, with an investment of 2 million euros.
- Municipality of Laveno-Mombello: creation of a cycling route along the lake, with an investment of 1.5 million euros.

These interventions aim to create an integrated, safe, and functional cycling network that can encourage the use of the bicycle as a daily means of transport, reducing traffic and pollution.

## Useful tools for your case
To verify your within/over 20 km tax scenario, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [tax return guide](nav:tax-return). ### What to Do

To take advantage of the new cycling paths funded by the Lombardy plan, it is essential to stay informed about ongoing projects and existing infrastructure. The Lombardy Region has allocated 115 million euros to develop a network of cycling paths that will connect major urban and rural centers, improving sustainable mobility. Among the most relevant projects are the Lake Como cycling path, which connects Como to Colico, and the Naviglio cycling path, which links Milan to Pavia, stretching 45 kilometers in length.

Citizens can consult the Lombardy Region's website for updates on the paths and available resources. Additionally, you can use our [calculator](nav:calculator) to discover the nearest cycling paths and plan your route. For example, residents of Lecco can use the new cycling path that connects the city center to Malgrate, a distance of approximately 10 kilometers, ideal for those who wish to avoid car traffic.

### Next Steps

The Lombardy Region is working to improve sustainable mobility and reduce environmental impact. Citizens can support this goal by using the cycling paths and promoting the use of bicycles as a daily means of transport. According to regional regulations, by 2025, all cities with more than 50,000 inhabitants must have a continuous and safe cycling network. This includes cities like Bergamo, Brescia, and Varese, which are already implementing new paths.

For those living near the Swiss border, such as residents of Mendrisio or Chiasso, it is important to note that Switzerland already has a well-developed cycling network. For example, the route connecting Lugano to Mendrisio is approximately 20 kilometers long and offers a panoramic view of Lake Lugano. Using these infrastructures can be an effective way to reduce car use and improve air quality.

### Operational Checklist

- **Inform**: Consult the Lombardy Region's website for updates on cycling paths.
- Plan: Use the [calculator](nav:calculator) to discover the nearest paths and plan your route.
- Explore: Try the new cycling paths and share your experiences with friends and family.
- Promote: Encourage the use of bicycles as a daily means of transport.

### Comparison of Practical Scenarios

- Before the cycling plan: Those who wanted to travel by bicycle from Milan to Pavia had to face busy and unsafe roads, covering approximately 45 kilometers.
- After the cycling plan: Thanks to the new Naviglio cycling path, the route has become safer and more scenic, stretching 45 kilometers with a well-maintained surface.

### Concrete Examples

- Lake Como: The new cycling path connecting Como to Colico offers a spectacular view of the lake and the surrounding mountains, stretching approximately 60 kilometers.
- Lecco: The cycling path connecting Lecco to Malgrate is approximately 10 kilometers long and offers a panoramic view of Lake Garda.

### Regulations and Dates

- Lombardy Regional Law n. 16/2020: Allocation of 115 million euros for the cycling plan.
- 2025: Deadline for the implementation of continuous and safe cycling networks in all cities with more than 50,000 inhabitants.

### Conclusion

Using the new cycling paths not only improves the quality of life but also helps reduce environmental impact. With the implementation of these infrastructures, the Lombardy Region is taking significant steps towards more sustainable and safe mobility for all citizens.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/la-lombardia-aggiorna-il-piano-ciclabile-115-milioni-per-25-grandi-percorsi/2560444/)*

item.title: Caslano defies forecasts with 311k CHF surplus
item.description: The municipality of Caslano closes 2025 with a surplus of 311,000 CHF, exceeding the forecasted deficit of 79,000 CHF.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Caslano closes 2025 with a surplus of 311,000 francs
- Budget forecasted a deficit of 79,000 francs
- Own capital rises to 5.2 million

## Key facts
- **What**: 2025 municipal budget
- When: 2025
- Where: Caslano, Ticino
- Who: Municipality of Caslano
- Amount: Surplus of 311,000 francs
- Forecast: Deficit of 79,000 francs
- Own capital: 5.2 million
- Revenues: Fiscal windfalls and supplementary taxes
- Expenditure: Contained with careful management measures

The Municipality of Caslano closed 2025 with a surplus of nearly 311,000 francs, reversing initial forecasts that predicted a deficit of just under 79,000 francs. This result, described as 'significantly better' by the local Municipality, allowed for the consolidation of own capital, which reached 5.2 million francs, confirming an economically and financially balanced situation.

The municipal budget benefited from significant fiscal windfalls and revenues from supplementary and special taxes. The Executive acted decisively to contain current expenditure where possible, adopting careful and targeted management measures. This prudent approach was considered fundamental in a context characterized by a generalized increase in consumer prices and costs related to social services.

The Municipality emphasized the importance of maintaining responsible and sustainable investment planning, thus ensuring the long-term financial stability of the Municipality and the quality of services offered to the population. Investment activity generated a net burden of 3.5 million. The dossier will be discussed by the Municipal Council on May 28th.

### Implications for cross-border workers

For cross-border workers residing or working in Caslano, this budget surplus could mean greater stability in municipal services and better planning for future investments. Careful management of municipal finances could translate into a greater offer of services and infrastructure, improving the quality of life for all residents, including cross-border workers.

### Comparison with initial forecasts

The initial forecasts of the Municipality of Caslano indicated a deficit of 79,000 francs for 2025. However, thanks to prudent management and unexpected revenues, the budget closed with a surplus of 311,000 francs. This reversal of forecasts is a positive sign for the community, demonstrating that prudent financial management can lead to better-than-expected results.

### Future scenarios

With consolidated own capital and a surplus budget, the Municipality of Caslano is in a better position to face any future challenges. Responsible investment planning could lead to infrastructural improvements and municipal services, benefiting both residents and cross-border workers living or working in the area.

### Procedure for budget discussion

The municipal budget dossier will be discussed by the Municipal Council on May 28th. This discussion will be crucial to defining future guidelines for the Municipality's financial management and to ensuring that funds are used effectively and sustainably.

For cross-border workers interested in following the discussion or participating, it is possible to contact the Municipality of Caslano for more information on how to access Municipal Council meetings. ## Analysis of Revenues and Expenses

### Unexpected Revenues

The municipal budget of Caslano has benefited from significant fiscal windfalls and substantial revenues derived from supplementary and special taxes. These unexpected revenues played a crucial role in reversing the initial deficit forecasts.

### Expense Management

The municipal executive has acted decisively to contain current spending where possible, adopting careful and targeted management measures. This prudent approach has been fundamental in a context characterized by a generalized increase in consumer prices and costs related to social services.

### Investment Planning

The Municipality has emphasized the importance of maintaining responsible and sustainable investment planning. This approach ensures the financial stability of the municipality and the quality of services offered to the population, including cross-border workers.

### Comparison with Other Municipalities

Compared to other municipalities in the Canton of Ticino, Caslano has demonstrated effective financial management, managing to close the budget with a surplus despite initially negative forecasts. This example could be followed by other municipalities to improve their financial management.

### Implications for Cross-Border Workers

For cross-border workers residing or working in Caslano, the careful management of municipal finances could translate into a greater offer of services and infrastructure, improving the quality of life. The financial stability of the municipality is a positive factor for all residents, including cross-border workers.

### Future Scenarios

With a consolidated own capital and a budget surplus, the municipality of Caslano is in a better position to face any future challenges. Responsible investment planning could lead to infrastructural improvements and municipal services, benefiting both residents and cross-border workers living or working in the area.

### Procedure for Budget Discussion

The municipal budget dossier will be discussed by the Municipal Council on May 28th. This discussion will be crucial to define the future guidelines for the financial management of the municipality and to ensure that funds are used effectively and sustainably.

For cross-border workers interested in following the discussion or participating, it is possible to contact the Municipality of Caslano for more information on how to access the Municipal Council meetings.

### Comparative Table

| **Element** | Initial Forecast | Actual Result |
|---------------------------|-------------------------|-------------------------|
| Deficit/Surplus | -79,000 francs | +311,000 francs |
| Own Capital | Not specified | 5.2 million francs |
| Unexpected Revenues | Not expected | Fiscal windfalls |
| Current Expenses | Not specified | Contained |
| Investments | Not specified | 3.5 million francs |

This table clearly shows how the initial forecasts were reversed thanks to careful financial management and unexpected revenues.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Concrete actions for cross-border workers

### Steps to participate in the budget discussion

1. **Contact the Municipality**: Cross-border workers interested in following the budget discussion can contact the Municipality of Caslano for more information on how to access the municipal council meetings.
2. Check the dates: Make sure to know the date and time of the municipal council meeting, scheduled for May 28th.
3. Prepare questions: Cross-border workers can prepare specific questions regarding the municipality's financial management and future investments.
4. Attend the meeting: Be present at the municipal council meeting to express your opinions and suggestions.
5. Stay updated: Follow local news and municipal announcements to stay informed about any future developments.

### Using the site's tools

For cross-border workers who wish to further explore the financial situation of the Municipality of Caslano, the [tax calculator](nav:calculator) available on the Frontaliere Ticino website can be used. This tool can help better understand the impact of municipal decisions on personal finances.

### Comparison with other situations

Cross-border workers can also compare the situation in Caslano with that of other municipalities in the Canton of Ticino using the [municipality comparator](nav:border-map). This tool allows you to see how other municipalities manage their finances and what services they offer to residents.

### Future planning

With careful financial management and responsible investment planning, the Municipality of Caslano is in a better position to face any future challenges. Cross-border workers can benefit from this financial stability, which could translate into a greater offer of services and infrastructure.

### Conclusion

In conclusion, the careful financial management of the Municipality of Caslano has led to a positive result, with a budget surplus exceeding initial forecasts. This is a positive sign for the community, including cross-border workers, and demonstrates the importance of responsible and sustainable planning. To stay updated and actively participate in municipal life, cross-border workers can follow the indicated steps and use the tools available on the Frontaliere Ticino website.
