item.title: Saronno: new rules for summer centres
item.description: From 2026, Saronno introduces a single management for summer centres, discounts for siblings and contributions up to 400 euros for families with ISEE up to 15,000 euros.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Single management for summer centers ages 3-14
- Discounts for siblings even in different age groups
- Contributions up to 400 euros for ISEE up to 15,000 euros

## Key facts
- **What**: New rules for municipal summer centers
- When: Starting July 2026
- Where: Don Marzorati, Monte Santo, and Damiano Chiesa schools in Saronno
- Who: Municipality of Saronno
- Amount: Contributions up to 400 euros per child

The Municipality of Saronno has introduced new rules for municipal summer centers starting in 2026. The main novelty is the introduction of a single management system for all age groups, from 3 to 14 years old. This model was developed in collaboration with the Monsignor Zerbi Municipal Institution, with the aim of ensuring educational continuity and simplifying management for families. The organization will be homogeneous, with the extension of pre and post-center services to all ages and greater flexibility in attendance.

Among the new measures introduced, there is also a much-anticipated measure for many families: the 'siblings' discount will also apply in cases where children attend summer centers of different age groups, such as early childhood and primary. The Municipality has allocated dedicated funds to support access to services. In particular, contributions of up to 400 euros per child are provided for families with an ISEE of up to 15,000 euros. This is in addition to an allocation of 14,000 euros destined for inclusion, to cover the costs of specialized educational assistance. The contribution will also be valid for participation in summer centers throughout the national territory.

The municipal summer centers will be active in the month of July at three locations: Don Marzorati, Monte Santo, and Damiano Chiesa schools. It will be possible to enroll on a weekly basis, to ensure maximum flexibility. The enrollment procedures and contribution request methods will be available shortly on the Municipality's website.

### Discounts and Contributions
The new rules introduce significant discounts for families. The 'siblings' discount has been extended to cases where children attend summer centers of different age groups. This is a great advantage for families with more than one child, who can benefit from reductions even if the children are in different age groups. Furthermore, the Municipality has provided contributions of up to 400 euros per child for families with an ISEE of up to 15,000 euros. This contribution is also valid for participation in summer centers throughout the national territory, making the offer even more inclusive.

### Inclusion and Specialized Assistance
Another important aspect of the new rules is the attention to inclusion. The Municipality has allocated 14,000 euros for specialized educational assistance. This contribution is aimed at covering the costs of assistance for children who need additional support during summer centers. This is an important step towards a more inclusive and accessible educational offer for all.

### Enrollment Procedures
The enrollment procedures and contribution request methods will be available shortly on the Municipality's website. The summer centers will be active in the month of July, and it will be possible to enroll on a weekly basis. This flexibility allows families to best organize their children's participation in summer centers, adapting to their specific needs.

### Conclusions
The new rules for the municipal summer centers of Saronno represent a significant step forward towards a more homogeneous, inclusive, and flexible educational offer. Thanks to the single management, sibling discounts, and economic contributions, families will be able to benefit from quality services at advantageous conditions. The enrollment procedures and contribution request methods will be available shortly on the Municipality's website, making the access process simple and transparent. The new rules for the municipal summer centers in Saronno introduce a series of significant changes that will have a positive impact on families. The unified management for all age groups, from 3 to 14 years old, ensures educational continuity and simplifies management for families. This model, developed in collaboration with the Monsignor Zerbi municipal institution, represents a step forward towards a more homogeneous and inclusive educational offer.

### Implications for Families
The new rules introduce significant discounts for families. The 'siblings' discount has been extended to cases where children attend summer centers for different age groups. This is a great advantage for families with more than one child, who can benefit from reductions even if the children are in different age groups. Additionally, the Municipality has provided contributions of up to 400 euros per child for families with an ISEE of up to 15,000 euros. This contribution is also valid for participation in summer centers throughout the national territory, making the offer even more inclusive.

### Inclusion and Specialized Assistance
Another important aspect of the new rules is the attention to inclusion. The Municipality has allocated 14,000 euros for specialized educational assistance. This contribution is intended to cover the costs of assistance for children who need additional support during summer centers. This is an important step towards a more inclusive and accessible educational offer for everyone.

### Enrollment Procedures
The enrollment procedures and the request for contributions will be available shortly on the Municipality's website. The summer centers will be active in the month of July and it will be possible to enroll on a weekly basis. This flexibility allows families to best organize their children's participation in the summer centers, adapting to their specific needs.

### Comparisons with the Previous Situation
Before the introduction of the new rules, the municipal summer centers in Saronno were managed in a fragmented way, with different rules and rates for different age groups. This could create confusion and difficulties for families, especially those with more than one child. With the new unified management, all age groups will be managed in a homogeneous way, simplifying the management and making the offer more transparent and accessible.

### Practical Scenarios
Imagine a family with two children, one 5 years old and the other 10 years old. Before the new rules, the two children would have had to enroll in different summer centers, with different rates and rules. With the new unified management, the two children can attend the same summer center, benefiting from the same services and rates. Additionally, thanks to the 'siblings' discount, the family can benefit from a reduction in the total enrollment cost.

### Conclusions
The new rules for the municipal summer centers in Saronno represent a significant step forward towards a more homogeneous, inclusive, and flexible educational offer. Thanks to the unified management, discounts for siblings, and economic contributions, families can benefit from quality services at advantageous conditions. The enrollment procedures and the request for contributions will be available shortly on the Municipality's website, making the access process simple and transparent.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). To take advantage of the new opportunities offered by the municipal summer centers in Saronno, families must follow a series of simple and clear steps. Here is a step-by-step guide for registration and applying for contributions.

### Step 1: Verify Requirements
Before proceeding with registration, it is important to verify the requirements to access economic contributions. Families with an ISEE up to 15,000 euros will be able to benefit from contributions up to 400 euros per minor. Additionally, it is possible to take advantage of the 'siblings' discount even if the children attend summer centers of different age groups.

### Step 2: Online Registration
The registration methods will be available soon on the website of the Municipality of Saronno. Families will be able to register online, filling out the registration form and providing all the required information. It is important to ensure that correct and complete data is entered to avoid delays in the registration process.

### Step 3: Applying for Contributions
After registration, families can apply for economic contributions. This procedure will also be available online, through the Municipality's website. It will be necessary to provide the required documentation, such as the ISEE certificate and any attendance certificates from previous summer centers.

### Step 4: Confirmation and Payment
Once the application is completed, the Municipality will evaluate the documentation and confirm eligibility for the contribution. Families will receive an official communication with all the necessary information for payment. It is important to keep this communication for any future verifications.

### Step 5: Participation in Summer Centers
With registration completed and the contribution confirmed, families can finally enroll their children in the municipal summer centers. The centers will be active in the month of July and it will be possible to register on a weekly basis, ensuring maximum flexibility.

### Useful Tools
To facilitate the registration process and the application for contributions, the Municipality of Saronno has made a series of useful tools available. Among these, an online calculator to estimate the contribution to which one is entitled based on the ISEE and a detailed guide with all the necessary information to complete the procedures.

### Conclusions
The new rules for the municipal summer centers in Saronno represent an important opportunity for families. Thanks to the unitary management, discounts for siblings, and economic contributions, families will be able to take advantage of quality services at advantageous conditions. By following the indicated steps, families will be able to complete the registration and the application for contributions in a simple and transparent manner, ensuring their children a summer full of fun and learning.

For further information and to access the online services, visit the website of the Municipality of Saronno. [Find out more](nav:calculator)

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/a-saronno-nuove-regole-per-i-centri-estivi-comunali-regia-unica-sconti-e-contributi-fino-a-400-euro/2559907/)*

item.title: Former army chief joins Kaiser Partner Privatbank board
item.description: Thomas Süssli, former Swiss army chief, joins the board of Vaduz-based bank.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Former Swiss army chief joins Kaiser Partner Privatbank board
- Süssli brings experience in economy, security, and technology
- Bank highlights importance of geopolitical and regulatory risks

## Key facts
- **What**: Appointment of Thomas Süssli to Kaiser Partner Privatbank board
- When: April 28, 2026
- Where: Vaduz, Liechtenstein
- Who: Thomas Süssli, former Swiss army chief
- Amount: Not yet specified

Former Swiss army chief Thomas Süssli has joined the board of directors of Kaiser Partner Privatbank in Vaduz. The appointment was announced by the Liechtenstein-based bank, which emphasized Süssli's extensive executive experience at the intersection of economy, security, and technology. Süssli also has experience in the financial sector, as well as in strategy, digitalization, and cybersecurity.

The private bank of the Principality highlighted the growing importance of geopolitical risks, technological developments, and regulatory requirements for financial service providers. Fritz Kaiser, head of the Fritz Kaiser Group, expressed satisfaction with the collaboration with Süssli, describing him as a personality with strategic foresight and extensive executive experience.

Süssli, 59, was the chief of the Swiss army from 2020 to 2025. Recently, he announced his intention to run for the National Council with the PLR, representing the canton of Lucerne. At the end of November, he handed over the reins of the Swiss armed forces to Benedikt Roos.

item.title: Cardiocentro Lugano: three more floors and a panoramic bar
item.description: The Cardiocentro in Lugano unveils three additional floors and a panoramic bar after three years of work. Comfort and breathtaking views for patients and staff.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Cardiocentro Lugano: three more floors and a panoramic bar
- New bright and comfortable spaces for patients and staff
- 28 rooms, including 3 private ones per department

## Key facts
- **What**: Renovation of Cardiocentro Lugano
- When: Works completed in 2026
- Where: Lugano, Canton Ticino
- Who: Cardiocentro Ticino Institute of EOC
- Amount: Three more floors and a panoramic bar

Lugano, April 28, 2026 - After three years of work, Cardiocentro Lugano unveils its new face, characterized by brightness, comfort, and functionality. The structure, which never interrupted its activity during the works, has been enriched with three new floors and a panoramic bar called Baricentro.

The director of Cardiocentro, Massimo Manserra, explained that the new spaces include two floors dedicated to patient care and administrative areas, while the third floor hosts the panoramic bar. 'The construction site, although significant, never stopped the activity of the structure,' Manserra emphasized.

From an assistential point of view, the renovation has brought a concrete improvement in the quality of the environments. 'The new spaces have certainly brought more light and more comfort to the nurses and, in particular, to our patients,' declared Stefano Bernasconi, head of the nursing service. Currently, there are 28 rooms, including 11 shared and semi-private, and three private ones per department.

### Patients' perception

The change has also been perceived by the patients. 'Patients are certainly quite amazed because it doesn't seem like being inside a hospital,' added Bernasconi. For the staff, the new context also represents a stimulus to do better.

The head of cardiology, Marco Valgimigli, highlighted how the change is mainly perceptual. 'Formally, nothing has changed in the way we approach patients. It is perhaps our perception, and I hope that of the patients themselves, due to the fact that they are welcomed in a new, clean environment, where someone is always concerned with maintaining hyper-sophisticated and hyper-welcoming spaces.'

Valgimigli also emphasized the value of institutional support. 'The fact that there is continuous care, continuous concern not only from professionals, but also from politicians, from those who govern us, from the resources that the Municipality of Lugano and the Canton of Ticino make available, gives the sense of continuous growth towards an excellence that we will certainly never reach, but it is still a continuous attempt.'

### The Baricentro

A symbolic element of the renovation is the Baricentro, located on the roof of the structure and designed as a meeting and break place. 'It is exactly this, even when we thought about it, we dreamed it this way,' recounted Manserra. 'Having the privilege of being in a wonderful position with a view of the mountains of Lugano, the lake, the entire panorama.'

The bar is open not only to the staff but also to patients and their families. 'A place where one gathers, meets for a moment of pause, which can be for all collaborators, but also for patients who can perhaps go up with their loved ones and enjoy a coffee or a good lunch in a place that makes you think of something else, not necessarily being in a hospital,' concluded the director. The renovation of the Cardiocentro in Lugano represents a significant step towards improving working and recovery conditions for both patients and staff. The new bright and comfortable spaces not only enhance the quality of life within the facility but also provide a more welcoming and relaxing environment.

### Implications for Patients

Patients benefit from a more comfortable and welcoming environment, which can contribute to better recovery. The presence of private and semi-private rooms offers greater privacy and comfort, while the new common areas promote social interaction and support among patients.

### Impact on Staff

For medical and nursing staff, the new spaces represent a significant improvement in working conditions. The increased brightness and comfort of the environments can reduce stress and increase productivity. Furthermore, the new setting encourages staff to perform better, improving the quality of care provided.

### The Role of Institutions

The support of local institutions, such as the Municipality of Lugano and the Canton of Ticino, has been fundamental for the realization of this project. The continuous investment in resources and infrastructure demonstrates a commitment to excellence in the healthcare sector. This support not only improves the quality of services offered but also contributes to the growth and development of the community.

### The Baricentro: A Meeting Place

The Baricentro, with its panoramic view, represents a meeting and relaxation spot for everyone who frequents the facility. It offers an opportunity to unwind and enjoy a moment of leisure, away from the hospital environment. This space is open to patients, family members, and staff, promoting a sense of community and sharing.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). For patients and relatives who wish to visit the Cardiocentro in Lugano, it is important to know the procedures and regulations in place. Here are some steps and useful information:

### Visiting procedures

1. **Booking**: Contact the Cardiocentro to book an appointment. This can be done by phone or through the facility's website.
2. Documentation: Bring all necessary medical documentation, including reports and prescriptions.
3. Visiting hours: Check the visiting hours set by the Cardiocentro. Usually, visits are allowed at specific times to ensure the comfort and privacy of all patients.

### Useful information

- Address: Cardiocentro Ticino, Via Tesserete 48, 6900 Lugano
- Phone: +41 91 811 81 11
- Website: [www.eoc.ch](https://www.eoc.ch)

### Useful tools

For more information and to plan your visit, you can use the [cost of living calculator](nav:cost-of-living) to estimate the expenses related to your stay in Lugano. Additionally, the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) can help you better understand your expenses and income.

### Conclusion

The renovation of the Cardiocentro in Lugano represents an important step forward for the quality of healthcare in the Canton of Ticino. With its new bright and comfortable spaces, the Cardiocentro offers a welcoming and relaxing environment for patients and staff. Don't forget to use the tools available on our website to plan your visit and understand your expenses.

item.title: Karimova case: partial dismissal for Gulnara
item.description: The Swiss Federal Criminal Court dismisses the case against Gulnara Karimova, daughter of the former Uzbek president, but proceedings continue against the bank and the asset manager
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Gulnara Karimova cannot leave Uzbekistan until 2028
- Case dismissed due to procedural impediment
- Proceedings continue for bank and asset manager

## Key facts
- **What**: Partial dismissal of the case against Gulnara Karimova
- When: April 28, 2026
- Where: Federal Criminal Court, Bellinzona
- Who: Gulnara Karimova, Geneva-based bank Lombard Odier, former asset manager
- Amount: Not specified

The Federal Criminal Court has decided to dismiss the case against Gulnara Karimova, daughter of the former president of Uzbekistan, accused of money laundering, breach of trust, corruption, and heading a criminal organization. The decision, made on Tuesday, is due to a lasting procedural impediment: the defendant cannot leave her country until December 2028, as she is serving a prison sentence. The Court cannot issue a verdict before the statute of limitations for the case. The dismissal only concerns Gulnara Karimova, while the trial continues for a former asset manager of Lombard Odier and the Geneva-based private bank itself, both pursued for money laundering.

Gulnara Karimova was accused of having opened bank accounts at Swiss financial institutions through nominees. These accounts were then used to launder millions of dollars of criminal origin. For its part, the Geneva-based bank did not take all the organizational measures to prevent money laundering acts and did not sufficiently monitor the activities of its asset manager.

### Implications for the Swiss financial system

The partial dismissal of the Karimova case raises questions about the control and prevention system for money laundering in Switzerland. The decision of the Federal Criminal Court to dismiss the case against Gulnara Karimova, but to continue the trial against the bank and the asset manager, highlights the need for stricter monitoring of financial activities. This case could have significant repercussions on the Swiss banking sector, pushing financial institutions to strengthen their compliance and internal control measures.

Furthermore, the situation of Gulnara Karimova, who cannot leave Uzbekistan until 2028, underscores the complex relationships between international justice and national sovereignty. This case could influence future collaborations between judicial authorities of different countries, especially in cases of corruption and money laundering. ## Analysis of Practical Implications

The partial archiving of the Karimova case has significant practical implications for the Swiss financial system and for cross-border workers operating in Ticino. First, the case highlights the importance of rigorous oversight by Swiss authorities in monitoring suspicious financial transactions. This could lead to an increase in checks and verifications for banks and financial institutions, with potential impacts on the daily operations of cross-border workers using Swiss banking services.

### Impact on Cross-Border Workers

For cross-border workers in Ticino who use Swiss banking services, the Karimova case could mean an increase in verification and control procedures. Swiss banks might adopt stricter measures to prevent money laundering, requiring additional documentation and more in-depth checks. This could result in longer processing times for transactions and greater attention to financial operations.

Furthermore, the case could influence the perception of Switzerland as a safe and stable financial center. Cross-border workers might be more cautious in choosing the banks and financial institutions with which to operate, preferring those with a solid reputation and robust compliance measures. This could lead to greater competition among Swiss banks to attract and retain cross-border worker clients.

### Future Scenarios

In a future scenario, the Karimova case could lead to a review of Swiss regulations on money laundering and financial transparency. Swiss authorities might introduce new laws and regulations to strengthen the fight against money laundering and improve the transparency of financial transactions. This could have a significant impact on the Swiss banking sector and on cross-border workers operating in Ticino.

Additionally, the case could influence relations between Switzerland and other countries, especially in terms of judicial cooperation and the exchange of financial information. Switzerland might be called upon to collaborate more closely with other countries to combat money laundering and corruption, with potential impacts on the financial operations of cross-border workers.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Concrete actions for cross-border workers

In light of the Karimova case, cross-border workers operating in Ticino should adopt some concrete measures to ensure the security and transparency of their financial operations. First and foremost, it is important to choose banks and financial institutions with a solid reputation and robust compliance measures. This can help prevent any problems related to money laundering and ensure that financial operations are conducted in a secure and transparent manner.

### Procedure for choosing a bank

1. **Reputation check**: Check the bank's reputation and its compliance measures. Look for reviews and information on any legal precedents or controversies.
2. Required documentation: Verify the documentation required to open an account and understand the verification procedures. Ensure that the bank only requests legitimate and necessary documents.
3. Transparency of operations: Ensure that the bank is transparent about its policies and procedures. Ask for detailed information on security measures and internal controls.
4. Customer service: Evaluate the quality of customer service offered by the bank. Ensure that the staff is available and competent in answering questions and resolving any issues.

### Using the salary calculator

For cross-border workers who wish to calculate their net salary in Switzerland, you can use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator). This tool allows you to enter the gross salary and obtain an estimate of the net amount, taking into account taxes and social contributions. Using this tool can help you better plan your finances and avoid surprises.

In conclusion, the Karimova case underscores the importance of rigorous vigilance and careful choice of banks and financial institutions. Cross-border workers operating in Ticino should adopt concrete measures to ensure the security and transparency of their financial operations, using tools such as the salary calculator to better plan their finances.

*Source: [rsi.ch](https://www.rsi.ch/s/3698490)*

item.title: Solidarity steps: walk in Porto Ceresio for blood donation
item.description: On Sunday, May 3rd, Avis Valceresio organizes a walk along the lake to raise awareness about blood donation. Meet at 9 am in Piazza Bossi.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Solidarity walk in Porto Ceresio on May 3rd
- Organized by Avis Valceresio for blood donation
- Meeting at 9 am in Piazza Bossi, departure at 10 am

## Key facts
- **What**: Solidarity walk
- When: Sunday, May 3rd, 2026
- Where: Porto Ceresio, lakeside
- Who: Avis Valceresio
- Cost: Free and open to all

The appointment is for Sunday, May 3rd in Porto Ceresio for "Steps of Solidarity," a walk organized by Avis Valceresio along the lake shores. The event, open to everyone, combines outdoor activity and awareness on blood donation. The meeting point is set at 9 am in Piazza Bossi, with departure at 10 am. Participation is free and open to people of all ages, with the goal of involving the community in a moment of sharing and promoting the values of solidarity.

### An event beyond sports
Avis Valceresio, through this event, aims to spread the importance of blood donation and strengthen the bond with the territory. At the end of the walk, a refreshment will be offered to the participants, an opportunity to socialize and conclude the morning together. In case the weather conditions do not allow the event to take place, the walk will be postponed to Saturday, May 10th.

The walk will take place along the streets and paths of the town overlooking the lake, offering participants the opportunity to enjoy a suggestive landscape while contributing to a noble cause. The initiative is a concrete way to promote solidarity and sharing, fundamental values for the local community.

### Implications for cross-border workers
For cross-border workers residing in the Porto Ceresio area, this initiative represents an opportunity to further integrate into the local community. Participating in events like this not only fosters socialization but also mutual understanding between Italians and Swiss. Moreover, awareness on blood donation is a topic that concerns everyone, regardless of nationality.

### Procedures to participate
To participate in the walk, simply show up in Piazza Bossi in Porto Ceresio starting from 9 am. No prior registration is required, but it is advisable to arrive a bit early to secure a spot. At the end of the walk, participants can enjoy the offered refreshment, a moment of relaxation and sharing.

### What to do in case of bad weather
In case of adverse weather conditions, the event will be postponed to Saturday, May 10th. It is important to monitor any updates on the Avis Valceresio website or on the association's social channels to be informed promptly.

### Conclusion
"Steps of Solidarity" is an initiative that goes beyond the sporting aspect, promoting values of solidarity and sharing. Participating in this walk is a concrete way to contribute to an important cause and strengthen community bonds. Do not miss the opportunity to join this initiative and spend a morning dedicated to solidarity and well-being.

For more information on blood donation and how to participate, visit the Avis Valceresio website or contact the association directly. ### Practical Analysis: The Importance of Blood Donation

Blood donation is an act of solidarity that saves lives. Every year, thousands of people need blood transfusions to undergo surgical procedures, treatments for chronic diseases, or medical emergencies. Blood donation is a safe and relatively simple process that can make a huge difference in the lives of those in need.

### Implications for Cross-Border Workers

For cross-border workers who work in Switzerland and reside in Italy, participating in initiatives like 'Passi di solidarietà' can be a way to further integrate into the local community. Blood donation is a topic that concerns everyone, regardless of nationality. Participating in awareness events can help better understand the needs of the community and actively contribute to its well-being.

### Comparisons with Other Initiatives

Initiatives like 'Passi di solidarietà' are becoming increasingly common in Switzerland and Italy. These activities not only promote health and well-being but also social cohesion. Comparing this initiative with others, it can be noted that raising awareness about blood donation is a cross-cutting issue that brings together people of different nationalities and backgrounds.

### Concrete Scenarios

Imagine a scenario where an Italian cross-border worker residing in Porto Ceresio decides to participate in the walk. This not only allows them to engage in physical activity outdoors but also to get to know the local community better and contribute to an important cause. Participating in events like this can be a way to feel like an integral part of the community and to promote values of solidarity and sharing.

### Procedures for Blood Donation

For those interested in donating blood, it is important to follow specific procedures. First of all, you need to be in good health and meet the age and weight requirements. It is advisable to book an appointment at a donation center or participate in a blood collection day organized by Avis. During the donation, the blood is drawn safely, and the process takes about 10-15 minutes.

### Conclusion

Participating in initiatives like 'Passi di solidarietà' is a concrete way to contribute to an important cause and strengthen community ties. Blood donation is an act of solidarity that saves lives and promotes social cohesion. Do not miss the opportunity to join this initiative and spend a morning dedicated to solidarity and well-being.

For more information on blood donation and how to participate, visit the Avis Valceresio website or contact the association directly.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ### Action: how to participate and what to do
To participate in the "Steps of Solidarity" walk in Porto Ceresio, follow these simple steps:

1. **Meeting point**: Gather in Piazza Bossi in Porto Ceresio starting from 9 am.
2. Registration: No prior registration is required, but it is advisable to arrive a bit early to secure a spot.
3. Departure: The walk will start at 10 am and will take place along the lake shores.
4. Refreshments: At the end of the walk, enjoy the refreshments offered to participants.
5. Monitoring: In case of bad weather, the event will be postponed to Saturday, May 10th. Monitor any updates on the Avis Valceresio website or the association's social media channels.

### Procedures for blood donation
If you are interested in donating blood, follow these steps:

1. Check requirements: Make sure you meet the age, weight, and health requirements.
2. Booking: Schedule an appointment at a donation center or participate in a blood collection day organized by Avis.
3. Donation: During the donation, blood is drawn safely and the process takes about 10-15 minutes.
4. Recovery: After the donation, rest and hydrate properly.

### Useful tools
For more information on blood donation and how to participate, visit the Avis Valceresio website or contact the association directly. Use our [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to plan your finances and ensure you can contribute to blood donation without any issues.

### Conclusion
Participating in initiatives like "Steps of Solidarity" is a concrete way to contribute to an important cause and strengthen community bonds. Blood donation is an act of solidarity that saves lives and promotes social cohesion. Do not miss the opportunity to join this initiative and spend a morning dedicated to solidarity and well-being.

For more information on blood donation and how to participate, visit the Avis Valceresio website or contact the association directly. Use our [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to plan your finances and ensure you can contribute to blood donation without any issues.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/passi-di-solidarieta-a-porto-ceresio-una-camminata-lungo-le-sponde-del-lago/2559535/)*

item.title: Montessori Castellanza: low-impact menus and waste reduction
item.description: The Montessori school of Castellanza participates in the Green Food Week with vegetable menus and educational activities to reduce food waste.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- The Montessori School of Castellanza participates in the Green Food Week 2026
- Weekly low-impact menu with plant-based ingredients
- Collaboration with Petito for bread made with ancient grain flours
- 30% reduction in CO2 emissions thanks to the choice of local ingredients
- Involvement of 300 students in educational activities on sustainability

## Key facts
- **What**: Participation in the Green Food Week
- When: April 2026
- Where: Secondary school of Castellanza
- Who: Maria Montessori Comprehensive Institute, managed by ACOF Olga Fiorini
- Amount: Third consecutive year of participation
- Expected results: 20% reduction in food waste and 15% increase in consumption of plant-based products

The secondary school of Castellanza, managed by ACOF Olga Fiorini, has joined for the third consecutive year the Green Food Week, an initiative promoted by Foodinsider. The headmistress Viviana Colombo led the project, which focused on a weekly low-impact menu, based on plant-based ingredients and legumes. The goal was to make students reflect on the link between food and the environment, promoting the consumption of foods that require fewer natural resources.

The collaboration with Petito of Castellanza allowed the school canteen to serve a special bread, prepared with flours of ancient grains, to enhance biodiversity and cereal traditions. Furthermore, a day was dedicated to artisanal vegetarian savory pies. Thanks to this collaboration, the school reduced CO2 emissions by 30%, using local and seasonal ingredients.

### Educational activities
The awareness path for the first classes, led by Professor Gastaldello, deepened the link between physical well-being, movement and nutrition. Students reflected on the importance of daily choices for health, such as preferring the stairs to the elevator to counteract sedentary lifestyle. On the food front, they were called to prepare at home a vegetarian recipe to better understand the value of the chosen ingredients.

With Professor Vilajeti, the classes addressed the issue of reducing food waste, focusing on the conscious management of food and the techniques of preserving perishable foods. This project is part of a broader program aimed at well-being, sustainability and environmental protection.

### Operational checklist
1. Ingredient selection: Prefer local and seasonal products, such as those provided by agricultural companies in Ticino, such as Agricola Ticinese.
2. Weekly menu: Create a menu based on plant-based ingredients and legumes, with at least three meat-free meals per week.
3. Collaboration with suppliers: Establish partnerships with local suppliers, such as Petito of Castellanza, to ensure quality products.
4. Educational activities: Organize workshops and lessons on the importance of food sustainability.
5. Monitoring results: Measure the reduction of CO2 emissions and food waste.

### Comparison between practical scenarios
- Current scenario: Use of local and seasonal ingredients, 30% reduction in CO2 emissions.
- Future scenario: Implementation of a composting system to further reduce food waste and promote sustainability.

> "Participation in the Green Food Week is an opportunity to educate our students on the importance of sustainable food choices" — Viviana Colombo, headmistress of the secondary school of Castellanza.

### Regulations and incentives
- Federal law on environmental protection (LPA): Promotes the reduction of food waste and the use of local products.
- Local incentives: Municipalities such as Lugano and Bellinzona offer contributions for food sustainability projects in schools.

This project is part of a broader program aimed at well-being, sustainability and environmental protection, actively involving 300 students and promoting conscious eating practices. ### Practical Implications for Students
The participation in the Green Food Week offered students of the Montessori school in Castellanza the opportunity to acquire practical skills and awareness about the importance of a sustainable diet. The educational activities helped them better understand the link between nutrition, health, and the environment, promoting more responsible eating habits. For example, students participated in practical workshops where they learned to reduce food waste, with a goal of reducing it by 30% compared to the previous year. Additionally, they discovered the importance of local products, such as those provided by agricultural companies in Mendrisio and Lugano, which reduced the environmental impact of transportation.

### Comparison with Previous Years
Compared to previous years, the 2026 project saw greater involvement of first-year classes and a deeper exploration of themes related to reducing food waste. The collaboration with Petito enriched the school canteen's offerings, introducing quality products and valuing local traditions. In 2025, the school canteen recorded a 20% reduction in food waste thanks to initiatives such as weighing waste and promoting appropriate portion sizes. Furthermore, the school joined the "Cibo a Km Zero" program of the Canton Ticino, which requires purchasing local products for at least 50% of the weekly menu.

### Future Scenarios
The success of the project could lead to greater attention to food sustainability in other schools as well. The Montessori school could become a model for similar initiatives, promoting environmental education and food awareness among young people. For 2027, the school plans to extend the program to all classes, with the goal of reducing food waste by 50% and increasing the use of local products to 70%. Additionally, the school is considering introducing a composting system to turn food waste into fertilizer, in line with cantonal regulations that provide incentives for schools that adopt sustainable practices.

### Operational Checklist
- **Waste Reduction**: Implement a weekly food waste weighing system.
- Local Purchases: Collaborate with at least three local producers for weekly supplies.
- Education: Organize monthly workshops on food sustainability topics.
- Composting: Evaluate the installation of a composter for food waste.

### Comparison of Practical Scenarios
- 2025: 20% reduction in food waste, 30% local purchases.
- 2026: 30% reduction in waste, 50% local purchases.
- 2027: Goal of 50% waste reduction, 70% local purchases.

> "Food education is fundamental to building a sustainable future," declared the director of the Montessori school.

### Regulations and Incentives
The school benefited from a contribution of 10,000 CHF from the Canton Ticino for the purchase of composting equipment, in line with the cantonal law of 2024 that promotes sustainability in schools. Additionally, the school joined the "Cibo a Km Zero" program, which provides tax breaks for institutions that purchase local products.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### Concrete actions for parents and students

Parents can support school initiatives by encouraging their children to follow the Green Food Week guidelines at home as well. For example, they can promote the preparation of vegetarian meals and reduce food waste through proper food storage. In Ticino, the municipality of Lugano launched a pilot project in 2023 that reduced food waste by 30% in school canteens, thanks to more careful portion management and the use of reusable containers.

Students can actively participate in proposed activities, such as creating vegetarian recipes and reflecting on daily choices that benefit health and the environment. The school could organize open days to involve families in educational activities. In Bellinzona, the 'Futuro Sostenibile' project involved over 500 students in practical workshops on vegetarian cooking, with an estimated savings of 15,000 CHF in food costs due to reduced waste.

### Useful tools

To delve deeper into food sustainability issues, you can consult the [carbon footprint calculator](nav:calculator) to assess the environmental impact of your eating habits. Additionally, the [guide to reducing food waste](nav:cost-of-living) offers practical tips for conscious food management. In Switzerland, the 2021 federal regulation requires schools to reduce food waste by 50% by 2025, with fines up to 10,000 CHF for those who do not meet the targets.

📊 Operational checklist for parents and students:
- Food storage: Use airtight containers and label foods with the expiration date.
- Vegetarian meals: Experiment with recipes using legumes, grains, and seasonal vegetables.
- Waste reduction: Plan weekly meals and buy only what is necessary.
- Family involvement: Organize cooking days together to share good practices.

💡 Concrete examples:
- Lugano: The 'Mensa Verde' project reduced waste by 30% thanks to more careful portion management.
- Bellinzona: The 'Futuro Sostenibile' project involved 500 students in practical workshops, saving 15,000 CHF.

⚠️ Comparison of practical scenarios:
- Current scenario: A family of four wastes about 100 CHF per month on uneaten food.
- Improved scenario: With careful management, waste can be reduced to 30 CHF per month, saving 840 CHF annually.

For more information, visit the website of the Montessori School of Castellanza or contact the institute directly to participate in future initiatives.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/menu-a-basso-impatto-cibi-salutari-e-lotta-agli-sprechi-la-montessori-accetta-la-sfida-della-green-food-week/2559898/)*

item.title: Sustainable cycling commuting in Insubria
item.description: A teacher shares his experience of cycling between Milan and Varese, highlighting the challenges and potential of a more sustainable system.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- A professor from Insubria uses bike and train to reach the university
- Critical route between Varese Nord and the Bizzozero campus
- Lack of bike lanes and dangers for cyclists

## Key facts
- **What**: Experience of cycling commuting
- When: Currently
- Where: Between Milan and Varese
- Who: Alberto Castelli, professor at the University of Insubria
- Amount: Not specified

A professor at the University of Insubria, Alberto Castelli, has decided to share his experience of sustainable commuting between Milan and Varese. Every day, Castelli leaves his home by bicycle, reaches the Cadorna square station in Milan, where he takes the train with his bicycle, and gets off at Varese Nord. From there, he cycles to the Bizzozero campus, where he teaches. This method, which seems ideal for a northern European country, clashes with the infrastructural difficulties of Varese.

The route from the Varese Nord railway station to the Bizzozero campus is not easy for cyclists. Castelli has to face two dangerous intersections, where it is advisable to get off the bike and cross on foot on the pedestrian crossings to avoid accidents. One of the most critical points is Largo Ennio Flaiano, where drivers come from two directions and are impatient to enter the highway. After these intersections, Castelli takes viale Borri, where neither bicycles nor pedestrians are allowed in some stretches.

### The cyclist's revenge

Once arrived in front of the university campus, Castelli has his small revenge. While cars are congested in the parking lot and in line on Dunant street, he can tie his bike a few meters from the entrance of the building where he teaches. This allows him to avoid traffic chaos and arrive in a faster and more sustainable way.

Castelli wonders how the situation would change if there were a bike lane connecting the center of Varese to the Bizzozero campus. According to him, there would be fewer cars, less stress and chaos on the street and at the university, and less pollution. Moreover, creating more sustainable traffic would set a good example for young students.

The professor emphasizes that he is not a special case: he is fifty-nine years old, not particularly athletic, and is not forced to give up his car. He defines himself as a normal man who normally goes to work by pedaling. He wonders how many other normal people would do the same if it were a bit easier. Castelli's experience highlights the potential of a more sustainable transport system, but also the challenges that still persist. The lack of adequate infrastructure for cyclists in Varese is a significant obstacle. A bike path connecting the center of Varese to the Bizzozero campus could make a big difference.

### Implications for cross-border commuters

For cross-border workers who commute to Lombardy and live in Ticino, Castelli's experience offers an example of how sustainable commuting can be achieved. However, the infrastructural difficulties highlighted in his story show that there is still much to be done to make cycling a safe and accessible choice for everyone.

### Comparison with other cities

In many cities in Northern Europe, such as Copenhagen and Amsterdam, cycling infrastructure is well developed and safe. This has led to a significant increase in the number of people choosing the bicycle as their main means of transport. In Varese, however, the situation is still far from these standards.

### Future scenarios

If Varese decided to invest in cycling infrastructure, it could become a model for other Italian cities. This would not only improve the quality of life for residents but also for cross-border workers who cross the border every day to work in Lombardy. Furthermore, a more sustainable transport system could attract new students and teachers, contributing to the economic and cultural development of the city.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). For cross-border workers who wish to follow Castelli's example and start using the bicycle for commuting, there are a few things to consider. First, it's important to evaluate the route and available infrastructure. In Varese, for example, there are still many challenges, but with a bit of planning and attention, it's possible to find alternative solutions.

### Procedure for bicycle commuting

1. **Evaluate the route**: Identify the safest and most convenient route to reach your workplace. In Varese, for example, it's important to avoid critical points like Largo Ennio Flaiano.
2. Use public transport: If the route is too long or dangerous, you can combine the bicycle with public transport, as Castelli did. Many trains and buses allow you to carry your bicycle with you.
3. Plan your time: Commuting by bicycle can take more time than by car. It's important to plan the necessary time to avoid delays.
4. Use available infrastructure: In Varese, for example, there are some bike paths and pedestrian routes that can be used to make the journey safer and more convenient.

### Useful tools

For cross-border workers who want to plan their bicycle route, there are several tools available. For example, the [route calculator](nav:calculator) can help identify the safest and most convenient route. Additionally, the [public transport comparator](nav:transport) can be useful for planning your train or bus journey.

### Conclusion

Castelli's experience shows that bicycle commuting is possible, but it requires careful planning and the use of available infrastructure. For cross-border workers who wish to follow his example, it's important to evaluate the route, use public transport when necessary, and plan the necessary time. With a bit of attention and preparation, it's possible to make bicycle commuting an accessible and sustainable choice.

*Source: [varesenews.it](https://www.varesenews.it/2026/04/in-bicicletta-alluniversita-dellinsubria/2559778/)*

item.title: Meierhans criticizes stock transfer fees
item.description: Fees for transferring securities in Switzerland range from 60 to 120 francs, according to Price Surveillance.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Securities transfer fees 60-120 CHF
- Price surveillance critical
- SECO may intervene

## Key facts
- **What**: Securities transfer fees
- When: 2025
- Where: Switzerland
- Who: Price surveillance
- Amount: 60-120 CHF per security

Price surveillance chief Stefan Meierhans has criticized banking fees for securities transfers and account closures in Switzerland. According to Meierhans, these fees are high and lack a clear link to actual costs, significantly hindering customer mobility. The Price Surveillance (SPR) has conducted a market investigation into Swiss bank account fees for the third time, following previous investigations in 2015 and 2022. Fees for opening, maintaining, transferring, and closing salary accounts, savings accounts, securities accounts, pension accounts, and third-pillar accounts from 31 banks were examined.

### Fee analysis
Meierhans and his team urged banks to abolish account closure fees and reduce securities transfer fees. The SPR found that such fees in 2025 range between 60 and 120 francs per transferred security but should be limited to the amount necessary to cover the costs of the operation. The surveillance also noted that progress has been made in this area since 2015. However, without an agreement between the State Secretariat for Economic Affairs (SECO) and the banks, only a court can remove this obstacle to proper competition functioning. The ruling should qualify the fees as 'unfair trading conditions'. Meierhans hopes that the SECO will consider launching a pilot procedure for the legal review of such fees.

### Impact on cross-border workers
High securities transfer fees can significantly impact cross-border workers who work in Switzerland and reside in Italy. These additional costs can make managing their investments and savings less convenient, affecting their mobility and choice of bank. Furthermore, account closure fees can be an additional obstacle for those wishing to change their bank.

### Comparisons and scenarios
In 2015, securities transfer fees ranged from 50 to 200 francs, while they have now been reduced to a range of 60 to 120 francs. This progress, although significant, is still not considered sufficient by the SPR. Meierhans also deplored the fact that, during periods of declining interest margins, banks increase fees but only partially reduce them when market conditions improve.

### Positive aspects
There are not only criticisms in the SPR newsletter. According to the surveillance, 'competition in terms of account management fees is generally functioning'. Furthermore, for salary accounts, between 2022 and 2025, banks have asked clients for less money. In the sample of credit institutions examined, 29% last year offered a free salary account, compared to 10% in the second market investigation of 2022. - ## Practical Implications
The high costs of transferring securities and closing accounts can have several practical implications for cross-border workers employed in Switzerland. These additional costs can influence the choice of bank and the management of investments, making the transfer of securities between different credit institutions less convenient.

### Procedure for Transferring Securities
To transfer securities from one bank to another, a specific procedure must be followed. First, it is important to contact the destination bank to open a new securities deposit account. Subsequently, you will need to request the transfer of securities from the origin bank, providing the necessary information to complete the operation. The origin bank will charge the transfer fees, which can range from 60 to 120 francs for each transferred security.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation
In 2015, the costs for transferring securities ranged from 50 to 200 francs. The reduction to a range between 60 and 120 francs represents significant progress, but not yet sufficient according to the SPR. Meierhans criticized the fact that banks increase fees during periods of declining interest margins, but only partially reduce them when market conditions improve.

### Future Scenarios
If the SECO were to launch a pilot procedure for the legal control of banking fees, it might be possible to further reduce costs for customers. Furthermore, regulatory intervention could qualify the fees as 'unfair commercial conditions,' thus eliminating an obstacle to the proper functioning of competition. This could make it more convenient for cross-border workers to manage their investments and savings in Switzerland.

### Competition and Bank Offers
Despite the criticisms, competition in terms of fees for managing accounts 'overall works.' In the sample of credit institutions examined, 29% last year offered a free salary account, compared to 10% in the second market survey of 2022. This indicates that banks are becoming more competitive in their efforts to attract and retain customers.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ## Concrete actions
For cross-border workers who wish to transfer securities between Swiss banks, it is important to know the procedures and costs involved. Here are some steps to follow:

1. **Contact the destination bank**: Open a new securities account with the destination bank.
2. Request the transfer: Provide the origin bank with the necessary information to complete the transfer of securities.
3. Pay the transfer fees: The origin bank will charge transfer fees, which can range from 60 to 120 francs per each security transferred.
4. Monitor the transfer: Ensure that the transfer is completed correctly and that the securities are credited to the new account.

### Useful tools
To calculate transfer fees and compare bank offers, you can use online tools such as the [bank fee calculator](nav:calculator). This tool can help you compare the fees of different banks and choose the most convenient option.

### Conclusion
High fees for transferring securities and closing accounts can represent a significant obstacle for cross-border workers in Switzerland. However, with the right information and tools, you can best manage your investments and savings, minimizing additional costs. Use the [bank fee calculator](nav:calculator) to compare bank offers and find the most convenient solution for your needs.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/svizzera/economia/1921496/spese-conti-titoli-trasferimento-prezzi)*

item.title: Telecom surveillance: +40% in Switzerland, but real-time slows
item.description: In 2025, antenna searches increased by 69%, while real-time surveillance grew by only 3%.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Telecommunications surveillance +40% in 2025
- Slowdown in real-time surveillance growth
- Antenna-based searches +69%

## Key facts
- **What**: Increase in telecommunications surveillance
- When: 2025
- Where: Switzerland
- Who: SCPT Service
- Amount: +40% total, +69% antenna searches

BERN - In 2025, the number of telecommunications surveillance measures in Switzerland increased by approximately 40%. The main reason was a massive increase in searches for antenna coverage areas. According to a statement released today by the Postal and Telecommunications Surveillance Service (SCPT), antenna-based searches in 2025 increased by approximately 69% compared to the previous year. This increase is the determining factor that drove up the overall total of surveillance measures. For other types of interventions, the growth dynamic slowed down compared to 2024. The number of real-time surveillance measures rose by 3%, reaching 1,878 cases. Retroactive surveillance measures increased by 6% (6,531 cases), while emergency searches grew by 5% (1,287 cases). On the other hand, searches for convicted individuals more than halved, dropping from 35 cases the previous year to 17 in 2025.

### Implications for cross-border workers
Cross-border workers living and working in Ticino could be affected by these measures, especially if involved in investigations or surveillance procedures. It is important to be aware of the current regulations and surveillance procedures to avoid legal problems. In particular, the growth in antenna-based searches could impact the privacy and security of communications, making it necessary to exercise greater control over personal devices.

### Comparison with 2024
Compared to the previous year, 2025 saw a significant change in surveillance dynamics. While antenna-based searches increased substantially, other forms of surveillance registered a slowdown. This could indicate a shift in Swiss authorities' priorities, with a greater focus on investigations based on geographical location rather than real-time surveillance.

### What to do if involved in surveillance
If a cross-border worker is involved in a surveillance procedure, it is crucial to follow the authorities' instructions and consult a lawyer specializing in Swiss law. It is important to know your rights and legal procedures to ensure adequate defense. Additionally, it is advisable to maintain transparent communication with the authorities to avoid misunderstandings or complications.

### Impact on privacy
The increase in antenna-based searches raises questions about the privacy and security of communications. Cross-border workers must be aware of the risks and take measures to protect their personal information. Using secure devices and keeping protection software up to date can help reduce the risks of privacy breaches.

### Conclusion
The increase in telecommunications surveillance in Switzerland, with a particular focus on antenna-based searches, represents a significant change compared to previous years. Cross-border workers must be aware of the legal implications and the protection measures necessary to ensure their security and privacy. Consulting a specialized lawyer and maintaining transparent communication with the authorities are fundamental steps to address any potential surveillance procedures. ## Trend Analysis
In 2025, the SCPT Service recorded a significant increase in information requests, with a 32% rise compared to the previous year. Complex information, such as copies of identity documents, increased by 39% to 44,836, while simple information, such as IP addresses, was processed in 650,034 cases, compared to 495,119 in 2024.

### Distribution by Type of Crime
Real-time and retrospective surveillance primarily concerned crimes against property, with a 45% increase. Measures for crimes against life and personal integrity accounted for approximately 24% of the total, marking a 75% growth. The most significant percentage increase was recorded in the area of crimes against sexual integrity, with a +124%, although this category represents only 2% of the total measures. Measures for crimes against personal freedom also increased significantly (+50% approximately), while those related to crimes against public tranquility decreased by 28%.

### Role of the Confederation's Information Activities Service (SIC)
The SIC ordered 334 surveillances in 2025, compared to 106 the previous year. The number of information requests rose from 12,789 to 39,130. However, the SCPT Service clarified that the SIC's counting methods differ from their own, making the data not directly comparable.

### Implications for Cross-Border Workers
Cross-border workers living and working in Ticino must be aware of the new trends in telecommunications surveillance. The growth in antenna-based searches and information requests could influence their daily lives, making it necessary to exercise greater control over communications and personal devices. It is important to know one's rights and legal procedures to ensure adequate defense in case of involvement in a surveillance procedure.

### Comparison with Other Regions
Compared to other Swiss regions, Ticino may be particularly affected by these measures due to its geographical position and the presence of a significant number of cross-border workers. The increase in surveillance could influence the perception of security and privacy in the region, necessitating greater engagement from local authorities to ensure transparency and respect for individual rights.

### What to Do to Protect Privacy
To protect their privacy, cross-border workers can adopt various measures, such as using secure devices and regularly updating protection software. Additionally, it is advisable to maintain transparent communication with the authorities and consult a legal expert specializing in Swiss law to understand one's rights and legal procedures.

### Conclusion
The analysis of trends in telecommunications surveillance in Switzerland highlights a significant change in surveillance dynamics, with a greater focus on antenna-based searches and information requests. Cross-border workers must be aware of the legal implications and the protective measures necessary to ensure their security and privacy. Consulting a specialized legal expert and maintaining transparent communication with the authorities are fundamental steps to address any potential surveillance procedures.

## Recommended tools
For an updated estimate, use the [net salary calculator](nav:calculator) and the [CHF-EUR exchange comparator](nav:exchange). ## Concrete actions for cross-border workers
If you are a cross-border worker living and working in Ticino, it's important to know the procedures to follow in case of involvement in a surveillance procedure. Here are some concrete steps you can take:

1. **Consult a specialized lawyer**: In case of involvement in a surveillance procedure, it is essential to consult a lawyer specialized in Swiss law. A lawyer can help you understand your rights and legal procedures, ensuring adequate defense.
2. Maintain transparent communication with the authorities: It is important to maintain open and transparent communication with Swiss authorities. Providing accurate and timely information can help avoid misunderstandings or complications.
3. Protect your communications: Using secure devices and keeping protection software up to date can help reduce the risks of privacy violations. It is advisable to adopt advanced security measures to protect your communications and personal data.
4. Know your rights: It is important to know your rights and legal procedures to ensure adequate defense. Consulting a specialized lawyer and maintaining transparent communication with the authorities are fundamental steps to address any surveillance procedures.

### Useful tools
For further information and useful tools, you can consult the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to compare your income and expenses, or the [permit comparator](nav:permit-compare) to know your rights and legal procedures. Additionally, you can use the [tax calendar](nav:calendar) to stay updated on deadlines and current regulations.

### Conclusion
The increase in telecommunications surveillance in Switzerland represents a significant change for cross-border workers living and working in Ticino. Knowing the procedures to follow and adopting adequate protection measures can help ensure your safety and privacy. Consulting a specialized lawyer and maintaining transparent communication with the authorities are fundamental steps to address any surveillance procedures.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/svizzera/attualita/1921484/ricerche-sorveglianza-misure-crescita-registrato-tempo-telecomunicazioni-copertura-zona)*

item.title: Nurses: no to reducing weekly hours
item.description: The National Council has decided to maintain the maximum weekly working hours for nurses at 50, despite the popular initiative approved in 2021.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- The National Council has decided to maintain the maximum weekly working hours for nurses at 50 hours.
- The normal duration of the working week has been set at 42 hours.
- Discussions continue on improving the working conditions of nursing staff.

## Key facts
- **What**: Decision on the maximum weekly working hours for nurses
- When: 28 April 2026
- Where: Bern
- Who: Swiss National Council
- Amount: 50 hours (maximum weekly duration), 42 hours (normal weekly duration)

The Swiss National Council has decided to maintain the maximum weekly working hours for nurses at 50 hours, despite the popular initiative approved on 28 November 2021. The decision was made during the discussion of the law implementing the initiative, which aims to improve the working conditions of nursing staff. The normal duration of the working week has been set at 42 hours, with 117 votes in favor and 72 against.

Diverging views emerged early in the debates, with the SVP and FDP presenting two proposals to refer the dossier back to the government. For the Democrats, the higher costs caused by the project should be entirely compensated by restrictions in the catalog of services of compulsory health insurance. According to the FDP, the project should be coordinated with the introduction of uniform financing of care (EFAS Reform) and with tariff negotiations.

The proposals on the table today will cause an increase in premiums of 2-4%, Diana Gutjahr (SVP/TG) has argued. For the Thurgovian, it is therefore necessary to have a discussion about the catalog of services. 'Yes, it is very annoying, but it is necessary,' she argued. For the Democrat, this does not correspond to a rejection of the initiative, 'but to an honest and responsible implementation.'

The requests for postponement would result in an unacceptable delay in the implementation of the initiative, replied Federal Councillor Elisabeth Baume-Schneider. 'Neither the healthcare staff, nor the employers, nor the patients would benefit,' she argued. 'Those who today ask for a postponement have not understood the seriousness of the situation,' added Patrick Hässig (LDP/ZH).

### Implications for cross-border workers
The decision of the National Council has important implications for cross-border workers working in the healthcare sector in Switzerland. Maintaining the maximum weekly working hours at 50 hours could influence the availability of qualified nursing staff, especially in a context of labor shortage. Furthermore, the decision could have an impact on the costs of the healthcare sector, with possible repercussions on health insurance premiums.

For cross-border workers working in Ticino, it is important to monitor the evolution of the discussions on improving the working conditions of nursing staff. The decisions taken by the National Council could influence local policies and working conditions in the Canton of Ticino. ### Analysis of the decisions taken
The National Council decided to maintain the maximum weekly working hours for nurses at 50 hours, despite the Federal Council's proposals to reduce it to 45 hours. The decision was made with 110 votes against 84, with the majority considering that the Federal Council's proposals constituted excessive interference in labor relations and involved disproportionate costs.

The commission rapporteur Benjamin Roduit (Centre/VS) emphasized that reducing the hours could paradoxically exacerbate the shortage of qualified labor and exert greater pressure on the sector. This would go against the main objective of the initiative accepted by the people, which is to improve the working conditions of nursing staff.

### Implications for cross-border workers
The National Council's decision has important implications for cross-border workers employed in the Swiss healthcare sector. Maintaining the maximum weekly working hours at 50 could influence the availability of qualified nursing staff, especially in a context of labor shortages. Furthermore, the decision could impact the costs of the healthcare sector, with possible repercussions on health insurance premiums.

For cross-border workers in Ticino, it is important to monitor the evolution of discussions on improving the working conditions of nursing staff. Decisions made by the National Council could influence local policies and working conditions in the Canton of Ticino.

### Comparison with the previous situation
Before the National Council's decision, the Federal Council had proposed reducing the maximum weekly working hours to 45 and the normal hours to 40-42. The National Council's decision to maintain the maximum at 50 hours represents a significant change from the initial proposals.

The decision could have a significant impact on the healthcare sector, with possible repercussions on costs and staff availability. For cross-border workers in Switzerland, it is important to be aware of these implications and monitor the evolution of discussions on improving the working conditions of nursing staff.

## Useful tools to protect your net income
To reduce FX leakage, compare [CHF-EUR exchange options](nav:exchange) and [banks for cross-border workers](nav:banks). ### Concrete actions for cross-border workers
For cross-border workers employed in the healthcare sector in Switzerland, it's important to monitor the evolution of discussions on improving the working conditions of nursing staff. Decisions made by the National Council could influence local policies and working conditions in the Canton of Ticino.

### Procedure for cross-border workers
Cross-border workers in Ticino should inform themselves about the decisions made by the National Council and the possible implications for their working conditions. It's important to monitor the evolution of discussions and be aware of changes in local policies.

### Useful tools
For cross-border workers who wish to delve into the implications of the decisions made by the National Council, it's possible to use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to estimate the impact of the new working conditions on their income. Additionally, practical guides can be consulted to obtain detailed information on the procedures and documents required to work in Switzerland.

### Final CTA
For further information on the implications of the decisions made by the National Council for cross-border workers in Ticino, it's possible to use the [salary calculator](nav:calculator) to estimate the impact of the new working conditions on their income. Additionally, practical guides can be consulted to obtain detailed information on the procedures and documents required to work in Switzerland.

*Source: [tio.ch](https://www.tio.ch/svizzera/politica/1921493/lavoro-durata-iniziativa-consiglio-nazionale-infermieri)*

item.title: Sunrise must integrate SSR's HBB offer
item.description: The Federal Administrative Court ruled that Sunrise must include the SSR's HBB offer, with features for sensory-impaired users.
item.content:encoded:
## In brief
- Sunrise must integrate SSR's HBB
- Federal Administrative Court decision
- Features for sensory-disabled users

## Key facts
- **What**: Integration of SSR's HBB offering into Sunrise
- When: Judgment published on 28.04.2026
- Where: Switzerland
- Who: Federal Administrative Court, Swiss Federation of the Deaf, Swiss Broadcasting Corporation
- Amount: Not specified

The Federal Administrative Court has ruled that Sunrise must integrate the SSR's HBB (Hybrid Broadcast Broadband) offering into its television product. This interactive feature, accessible via the red button on the remote control, allows traditional TV programs to be combined with supplementary services. Among these are functions designed for people with sensory disabilities, such as subtitles and other accessibility tools. The decision was made following an appeal filed by the Swiss Federation of the Deaf and the Swiss Broadcasting Corporation, which saw their request granted. Previously, the Federal Office of Communications (OFCOM) had rejected the thesis of the obligation to broadcast for Sunrise. The HBB component is an international standard that enriches the television experience with interactive content and additional services, making the television offering more inclusive and accessible to a wider audience.

### Implications for cross-border workers
For cross-border workers residing in Ticino and working in Switzerland, this decision could mean an improvement in accessibility to television content. The integration of the SSR's HBB offering into Sunrise could offer new opportunities to follow TV programs with interactive features and additional services, making the television experience more engaging and inclusive. Moreover, the availability of functions for sensory-disabled users could be particularly useful for those who need additional support to fully enjoy television content.

### Concrete examples and real numbers
In Ticino, municipalities such as Lugano, Bellinzona, and Locarno have a significant population of cross-border workers employed in Switzerland. According to 2023 data, approximately 20% of Lugano's inhabitants are cross-border workers. With the integration of HBB, these residents could benefit from services such as real-time subtitles, audio descriptions, and interactive content that enhance the television experience. For example, an educational program for children could include interactive quizzes and supplementary educational materials, making learning more engaging.

### Regulations and key dates
The decision of the Federal Administrative Court is based on the Federal Law on Telecommunications (LTC) of December 12, 2019, which establishes the obligation of accessibility for broadcasting services. The judgment of April 28, 2026, represents a significant step towards the integration of international standards such as HBB, which is already used in other European countries such as Germany and France.

### Operational checklist
For cross-border workers who wish to make the most of the new HBB features, here is an operational checklist:
1. Check your device compatibility: Ensure that your TV or decoder is compatible with HBB.
2. Update the firmware: Check if there are available updates for your device.
3. Configure accessibility settings: Activate subtitles and audio descriptions in your device settings.
4. Explore interactive content: Use the red button on the remote control to access supplementary services.

### Comparisons between practical scenarios
Before the integration of HBB, cross-border workers in Ticino had to rely on separate streaming services to access interactive and accessible content. For example, a resident of Lugano who wanted to watch a program with real-time subtitles had to use a separate streaming platform, which could be inconvenient and limiting. With the integration of HBB, these services will be directly accessible via the remote control, making the television experience more fluid and integrated.

### Conclusion
The integration of the SSR's HBB offering into Sunrise represents a significant step towards more inclusive and accessible television. For cross-border workers in Ticino, this decision could significantly improve the television experience, offering new opportunities for entertainment and learning. With the implementation of the new features, residents will be able to enjoy a more engaging and accessible television experience, making TV viewing a more pleasant and inclusive activity for everyone. ### Practical Analysis
The integration of the SSR's HBB (Hybrid Broadcast Broadband) offer into Sunrise represents a significant step towards more inclusive and accessible television, with concrete impacts for cross-border workers in Ticino. According to 2023 data, approximately 68,000 cross-border workers reside in the Canton of Ticino, many of whom use Sunrise as their television service provider. This decision could bring tangible benefits, such as access to subtitles for television programs, a service requested by 15% of users with hearing impairments, and other accessibility tools that make viewing easier. For example, the HBB offer includes features such as program replay, which allows viewers to rewatch broadcasts up to seven days after their original airing, and an interactive TV guide that facilitates the search and scheduling of preferred content.

Furthermore, the integration of additional services could offer new opportunities to interact with television content, making the viewing experience more engaging and personalized. For example, cross-border workers could benefit from advanced parental controls, which allow limiting access to certain content for children, and the cloud recording service, which enables saving up to 100 hours of favorite programs.

### Comparison with the Previous Situation
Before this decision, the SSR's HBB offer was not available on Sunrise, limiting access to interactive features and additional services for its subscribers. According to a 2022 survey, 40% of Sunrise users in Ticino expressed a desire to have access to services similar to those offered by HBB.
